description: A 2.4-mile trail a mile from downtown Nevada City, centered on Hirschman's Pond — five acres of water beneath cliffs carved by 1800s hydraulic mining. The first stretch is built to ADA standards and crosses a 40-foot trestle; dogs on leash.
A walking trail off Pleasant Valley Road in Penn Valley — one of the outings in MUSE's 'Seasons in the GVNC Cultural District.'
A walking trail in Nevada City, reached from Via Colina Drive.
Truckee's regional park on Brockway Road — a recurring MUSE stop, from the annual Calendar of Events to 'The Culture of Food in Nevada County.'
A family-owned motel two blocks from downtown Nevada City, with themed rooms and an adventure bent — the office keeps a library of hiking and biking route info, and dogs are welcome in most rooms.
Szabo Vineyards' tasting room on Broad Street in Nevada City — 'Californian wine, with Hungarian roots,' in their words.
A cafe on Richardson Street in Grass Valley, featured in MUSE's 'Seasons in the GVNC Cultural District.'
An artists' collective and gallery on Donner Pass Road in Truckee — a stop in MUSE's '48 Hours in Truckee' and 'In Nevada County, the Arts Are Essential.'
A gallery space at 201 W Main Street in Grass Valley.
A letterpress print shop — 'the original social media,' as they put it — printing wedding suites, business cards, and stationery, by appointment.
